tier_1_regimens.notes: Combination of pemetrexed with either cisplatin or carboplatin. Extrapolated from pleural mesothelioma data (Vogelzang et al. [1]). EAP data in PeM showed ORR 25% first-line; median survival not reached. Carboplatin is recommended if patient is not a candidate for cisplatin. This regimen is category 2A, preferred for first-line treatment of epithelioid peritoneal mesothelioma. Reference: [1] Vogelzang NJ, et al. J Clin Oncol 2003;21:2636-2644; [6] Santoro et al. J Thorac Oncol 2008;3:756-763.

Addition of bevacizumab to platinum/pemetrexed. Based on the MAPS trial (Zalcman et al. [8]) in pleural mesothelioma. An FDA-approved biosimilar can substitute for bevacizumab. At least 6-week interval between last dose of bevacizumab and cytoreductive surgery (CRS). Category 2A, preferred first-line for epithelioid histology.

Combination of platinum/pemetrexed with pembrolizumab. Pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV pembrolizumab with different dosing and administration instructions. Based on phase 3 trial by Chu et al. (Lancet 2023 [10]). Category 2A, preferred first-line for epithelioid peritoneal mesothelioma.

trial_data.other_endpoints: 3-year outcomes reported by Peters et al. Ann Oncol 2022 [11]

tier_1_regimens.notes: Dual immune checkpoint inhibitor regimen. Nivolumab and hyaluronidase-nvhy subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV nivolumab (monotherapy only). PD-L1 testing not required. Based on CheckMate 743 trial in pleural mesothelioma (Baas et al. Lancet 2021 [107]). Category 2A, preferred first-line for epithelioid peritoneal mesothelioma.

Gemcitabine plus platinum regimen. Based on phase II trials in pleural mesothelioma (Nowak et al. [12], van Haarst et al. [13], Favaretto et al. [14]). Category 2A, useful in certain circumstances for first-line treatment of epithelioid peritoneal mesothelioma.

Single-agent pemetrexed. Based on EAP data (Taylor et al. [15]) showing median OS 10.3 months in first-line PeM. Category 2A, useful in certain circumstances for first-line epithelioid peritoneal mesothelioma.
Single-agent vinorelbine. Based on the MS01 trial (Muers et al. [16]) in pleural mesothelioma. Category 2A, useful in certain circumstances for first-line epithelioid peritoneal mesothelioma.

Nivolumab monotherapy for relapsed mesothelioma after platinum-based chemotherapy. CONFIRM trial (Fennell et al. [18]) showed median OS 10.2 vs 6.9 months, HR 0.69. Nivolumab and hyaluronidase-nvhy subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV nivolumab. PD-L1 testing not required. Category 2A, preferred subsequent therapy if chemotherapy was first-line.

Dual checkpoint inhibitor for relapsed mesothelioma. Based on MAPS2 (Scherpereel et al. [17]) and INITIATE (Disselhorst et al. [19]) phase 2 trials. Nivolumab subcutaneous injection not approved for concurrent use with ipilimumab. Category 2A, preferred subsequent therapy if chemotherapy was first-line.

If first-line immunotherapy (ipilimumab+nivolumab) is used, subsequent therapy with platinum/pemetrexed is preferred. Rechallenge with pemetrexed may be considered if good sustained response to prior pemetrexed. Category 2A, preferred subsequent therapy for epithelioid histology after Ipi+Nivo.

Platinum/pemetrexed plus bevacizumab after first-line immunotherapy. Bevacizumab biosimilar acceptable. At least 6-week interval before CRS. Category 2A, preferred subsequent therapy for epithelioid after Ipi+Nivo.

Single-agent pemetrexed as subsequent therapy. Based on phase III trial (Jassem et al. [20]) and retrospective survey (Zucali et al. [21]). Category 2A, other recommended subsequent therapy for epithelioid.

Combination of PD-L1 inhibitor and VEGF inhibitor. Phase 2 trial in advanced PeM (Raghav et al. [22]) showed ORR 40% and 1-year OS 85%. Only for patients not previously treated with immune checkpoint inhibitors. Atezolizumab and hyaluronidase-tqjs subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV atezolizumab. Category 2A, other recommended subsequent therapy for epithelioid.
Single-agent gemcitabine as subsequent therapy. Based on retrospective data (Manegold et al. [23], van Meerbeeck et al. [24]). Category 2A, other recommended subsequent therapy for epithelioid.
Single-agent vinorelbine as subsequent therapy. Based on retrospective studies (Stebbing et al. [25], Zauderer et al. [26]). Category 2A, other recommended subsequent therapy for epithelioid.

Preferred first-line regimen for biphasic/sarcomatoid histology. Based on CheckMate 743 (Baas et al. [107]), which showed benefit in non-epithelioid subgroup. Nivolumab SC substitution not approved with ipilimumab. PD-L1 testing not required. Category 2A, preferred for biphasic/sarcomatoid first-line.

Platinum/pemetrexed plus pembrolizumab. Pembrolizumab SC substitution possible. Based on phase 3 trial (Chu et al. [10]) which included both epithelioid and non-epithelioid (though data for non-epithelioid not specified). Category 2A, preferred first-line for biphasic/sarcomatoid.
Platinum/pemetrexed considered other recommended for biphasic/sarcomatoid first-line, as immunotherapy-based regimens are preferred. Category 2A, other recommended.
With bevacizumab, other recommended for biphasic/sarcomatoid first-line. Biosimilar acceptable. Category 2A.
Gemcitabine plus platinum, useful in certain circumstances for biphasic/sarcomatoid first-line.
Single-agent pemetrexed, useful in certain circumstances for biphasic/sarcomatoid first-line.
Single-agent vinorelbine, useful in certain circumstances for biphasic/sarcomatoid first-line.
Nivolumab monotherapy subsequent therapy after chemotherapy. CONFIRM data. SC substitution possible. Category 2A, preferred.
Dual checkpoint inhibitor subsequent therapy after chemotherapy. Category 2A, preferred.
Platinum/pemetrexed subsequent after Ipi+Nivo. Category 2A, preferred.
With bevacizumab, subsequent after Ipi+Nivo. Category 2A, preferred.
Single-agent pemetrexed other recommended for subsequent therapy.
Only for patients not previously treated with ICIs. Category 2A, other recommended.
Single-agent gemcitabine, other recommended.
Single-agent vinorelbine, other recommended.

Cisplatin 50 mg/L plus doxorubicin 15 mg/L of perfusate for 90 minutes. Reference 15 (Chicago Consensus). Platinum agents (cisplatin, carboplatin) associated with improved outcomes over mitomycin C in retrospective comparisons.
Alternative dosing per BSA. Reference 15.
Cisplatin monotherapy. Data from multi-institutional study (Yan et al. [76]) showing median OS 53 months. Dosing range 100-240 mg/m² for 90-110 minutes.
Carboplatin monotherapy. Reference 18 (Shetty et al.).
Cisplatin/mitomycin combination. References 19, 20.
Mitomycin monotherapy, considered useful in certain circumstances per Chicago Consensus.

drugs.name: Mitomycin
drugs.dose: 30 mg at time 0, then 10 mg at 60 minutes

tier_1_regimens.notes: Split-dose mitomycin regimen, useful in certain circumstances.
